title: pilosella rothiana
intro: Pilosella rothiana, also known as Alpine Mouse Ear or Roth's Hawkweed, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to the European Alps. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Pilosella rothiana include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Pilosella rothiana can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to digestive problems.
